# WhatsApp Reporting & Analytics — Design Spec

**Companion to the prototype at `/whatsapp/dashboard`.**
**Author:** Generated by Cursor agent, 2026-04-18.
**Design system source of truth:** Figma file `WhatsApp (Analytics)` (fileKey `oVVMotC92NoabJa6hBW3cp`). All tokens below were captured directly from rendered node fills and the Mailchimp theme layer of the Intuit Fusion/Oasis variable collections.

---

## 1. Foundations

### 1.1 Design tokens

All tokens are mirrored between `lib/tokens.ts` (the TypeScript source of truth) and `app/globals.css` (the CSS custom-property runtime). Change one and update the other.

#### Colors

| Token | Hex | Role |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `--mc-yellow` | `#FFE01B` | Cavendish Yellow — brand strip, logo accent |
| `--mc-whatsapp` | `#25D366` | WhatsApp channel glyph |
| `--mc-text-primary` | `#21262A` | Primary text (not pure black) |
| `--mc-text-secondary` | `#5D686F` | Secondary text, labels |
| `--mc-text-tertiary` | `#727E85` | Tertiary text, icons |
| `--mc-text-peppercorn` | `#241C15` | Headline / peppercorn contrast |
| `--mc-text-inverse` | `#FFFFFF` | On dark surfaces |
| `--mc-surface` | `#FFFFFF` | Card and panel background |
| `--mc-page` | `#F0F4F6` | App page background |
| `--mc-subtle` | `#F8FAFB` | Subtle container background |
| `--mc-border` | `#E2E9ED` | Default border / divider |
| `--mc-border-strong` | `#D5DEE3` | Button and input borders |
| `--mc-border-muted` | `#C3CED5` | Muted border |
| `--mc-cta-teal` | `#007C89` | Primary CTA fill |
| `--mc-cta-teal-hover` | `#006670` | Primary CTA hover |
| `--mc-link` | `#2B77CC` | Links + chart primary blue |
| `--mc-positive` / `-bg` | `#00892E` / `#DFF5E7` | Success, delivered, healthy |
| `--mc-negative` / `-bg` | `#B61A37` / `#FDE7EA` | Error, action-needed |
| `--mc-attention` / `-bg` | `#2B77CC` / `#E0EDFF` | Watch, informational |
| `--mc-opportunity` / `-bg` | `#A275FF` / `#EFE8FF` | AI/sparkle, opportunity |
| `--mc-warning-bg` | `#FFF4D6` | Warm inline banners |
| `--mc-chart-{1..8}` | `#2B77CC, #00892E, #A275FF, #D72792, #00B3C2, #95BF47, #5E8E3E, #017E89` | Data-viz categorical palette |

#### Typography

Stack: `"Graphik Mailchimp", "Graphik", "Inter", -apple-system, sans-serif`. Public prototype uses **Inter** (Graphik Mailchimp is proprietary).

Weights in use: **400** (Regular), **500** (Medium). No 700 observed in the Figma file.

| Role | Size/Line-height/Weight | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Hero | 28 / 36 / 500 | "Welcome message" |
| Page title & metric | 24 / 29 / 500 | "WhatsApp report", "$12,000" |
| Section title | 20 / 28 / 500 | "WhatsApp performance" |
| Label (large) | 16 / 24 / 400 | "Sent from" |
| Body | 14 / 20 / 400–500 | Paragraph text, nav, buttons |
| Small | 13 / 20 / 500 | Small controls |
| Micro | 12 / 16 / 400–500 | Table cells, KPI labels |
| Tiny | 11 / 15 / 400 | Uppercase eyebrows, badges |

#### Radii

`4, 6, 8, 12, 9999`. Default `6px` for buttons/inputs, `8px` for chart containers, `12px` for cards.

#### Spacing scale

`2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 16, 24, 32`. 2/4-based. Observed density: component-inline padding mostly 10–12px; card inset 16–24px.

#### Shadows

- `--mc-shadow-sm`: `0 1px 2px rgba(76,85,91,0.08)`
- `--mc-shadow`: `0 1px 3px rgba(76,85,91,0.12), 0 1px 2px rgba(76,85,91,0.08)`
- `--mc-shadow-lg`: `0 8px 24px rgba(76,85,91,0.16)`

### 1.2 Severity system

Four states, used on badges, card borders, chart bars, and agent alerts. This beats red/green because it guides behavior.

| State | Color | When to use |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `healthy` | `#00892E` / `#DFF5E7` | Metric meets or beats benchmark |
| `watch` | `#2B77CC` / `#E0EDFF` | Directional drift, not yet a problem |
| `opportunity` | `#A275FF` / `#EFE8FF` | Measurable gap with a known lever |
| `action-needed` | `#B61A37` / `#FDE7EA` | Blocking issue or value leak |

Computed severities come from `lib/semantic.ts#severityFromDelta` and `#computeDropSeverity`.

---

## 2. Component inventory

Every component in `components/` — props, states, a11y notes. Ordered by layer.

### 2.1 Shell

| Component | Props | States | A11y |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `AppShell` | `children` | — | Wraps `<main>`, no landmark pollution |
| `YellowStrip` | — | — | Decorative, `aria-hidden` |
| `TopBar` | — | scrolled | `<header>` landmark, search is labeled |
| `LeftNav` | — | active | `<nav aria-label="Primary">`, `Link` elements |
| `PlanSwitcher` | `campaignId` | active | `role="group"`, each button `aria-pressed` |
| `FreddieLogo` | `className` | — | `role="img"` with `aria-label="Mailchimp"` |

### 2.2 Filters

| Component | Props | States | A11y |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `FilterBar` | `initial?`, `sticky?` | open/closed popover | Each trigger `aria-haspopup="listbox"`; option list `role="listbox"`, options `aria-selected` |

Filter state is local-component today; URL-sync is a TODO (spec'd in §5 data contracts).

### 2.3 KPI

| Component | Props | States | A11y |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `KpiCard` | `kpi: KpiDef`, `showIcon?`, `className?` | — | Value is visible text; delta has tone + icon |
| `KpiStrip` | `title?`, `defaultLens?` | lens selection | — |
| `LensSwitcher` | `value`, `onChange` | active | `role="tablist"`, each option `role="tab"` + `aria-selected` |

### 2.4 Cards

| Component | Props | States | A11y |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `Card` | `className?`, `children` | — | Plain `<div>` — compose with `<section>` when needed |
| `CardHeader` | `title`, `subtitle?`, `action?` | — | Uses `<h3>` for title |
| `SeverityBadge` | `severity`, `label?`, `size?` | — | Color + dot + text (not color-only) |
| `CampaignHero` | `campaign: Campaign` | — | Semantic `<h1>`, dl/dt/dd for metadata |
| `AdaptiveFunnel` | `stages: FunnelStage[]`, `title?`, `subtitle?` | per-stage severity | Each bar has `aria-label` with value |
| `AINarrative` | `narrative`, `chips?` | — | Eyebrow text identifies AI origin |
| `InsightCard` | `insight: Insight`, `label?` | severity-tinted | — |
| `RecommendationCard` | `insight: Insight` | idle / staged | Button becomes `disabled` after Apply |

### 2.5 Charts

Both chart components wrap Recharts `ResponsiveContainer`. They take minimum viable data contracts so callers don't need Recharts knowledge.

| Component | Props | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `TrendChart` | `series: {id,label,data,color}[]`, `height?`, `yFormatter?` | Area + Line with gradient fill |
| `BarChart` | `data`, `xKey`, `bars`, `height?`, `horizontal?`, `colorByIndex?` | Supports multi-series and horizontal |

### 2.6 Tables

| Component | Props | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `RankedTable` | `columns`, `rows`, `keyFn`, `dense?` | Generic `<T>`. Sort indicators are visual only (hook in sorting when real data arrives). |
| `RecipientsTable` | — | Mirrors the Figma table pixel-for-pixel; pagination is client-only over mock data. |

### 2.7 Smart chips & rail

| Component | Props | States | A11y |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `SparkleChip` | `label`, `onClick?`, `active?` | active, hover | Regular `<button>`, full keyboard |
| `ChipGroup` | `title`, `chips`, `activeId?`, `onSelect` | — | `<h4>` for title |
| `RightRail` | `insight`, `onClose`, `onFollowUp?` | open/closed | `role="dialog"`, `aria-modal`, ESC closes, overlay click closes |
| `EvidencePanel` | `evidence: EvidenceRef[]` | — | Renders the correct chart per `evidence.id` |
| `ActionComposer` | `actions: Action[]`, `inline?` | idle / staged | Buttons fire Toast confirmations |
| `Toast` | `message`, `onDone`, `durationMs?` | auto-dismiss | `role="status"`, `aria-live="polite"` |

### 2.8 Agent

| Component | Props | States | A11y |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `ObjectiveBar` | `objective`, `campaign`, `dateRange` | — | — |
| `HealthScorecard` | — | per-metric severity | — |
| `ConversationCanvas` | — | typing / idle | Input `aria-label`; Send button labeled |
| `AgentTurn` | `turn: AgentTurn`, `onFollowUp?` | agent / user | Minimal markdown bolds; evidence rendered via `EvidencePanel` |
| `EvidenceRail` | — | — | — |

### 2.9 UI primitives

| Component | Props |
| --- | --- |
| `Button` | `variant: primary \| secondary \| ghost \| link`, `size: sm \| md` |
| `Pill` | `tone: positive \| attention \| warning \| negative \| opportunity \| neutral` |
| `Tabs` | `tabs`, `value`, `onChange`, `size?` |

---

## 3. Insight-object schema

The single biggest architectural decision. **One registry powers every surface.**

```ts
type Insight = {
  id: string;
  type: "anomaly" | "trend" | "benchmark-gap" | "forecast" | "recommendation";
  group: "reach-quality" | "engagement" | "conversation" | "flow-completion"
       | "revenue" | "cross-channel" | "recommendations";
  scope: {
    campaignId?: string;
    templateId?: string;
    segmentId?: string;
    geo?: string;
    sequenceId?: string;
    flowStep?: string;
  };
  metrics: Record<string, number>;
  summary: string;          // plain-language one-liner
  rationale: string;        // why / root cause
  confidence: "low" | "med" | "high";
  severity: "healthy" | "watch" | "opportunity" | "action-needed";
  evidence: EvidenceRef[];
  recommendedAction?: Action;
  estimatedImpact?: { metric: string; delta: number; unit: "pct" | "abs" | "usd" };
  followUps: string[];
};
```

Example JSON for every insight type:

```jsonc
// anomaly
{ "id":"ins_delivery_drop_in", "type":"anomaly", "group":"reach-quality",
  "scope":{"campaignId":"cmp_welcome_wa","geo":"IN"},
  "metrics":{"deliveryRate":78,"portfolioDeliveryRate":85},
  "summary":"Delivery rate in India is 7 pts below portfolio average.",
  "rationale":"78% of India sends were delivered vs 85% portfolio. …",
  "confidence":"high","severity":"opportunity",
  "evidence":[{"id":"ev_geo_heatmap","kind":"heatmap","title":"Delivery rate by country"}],
  "recommendedAction":{"kind":"create-segment","label":"Create IN cleanup segment","description":"…"},
  "estimatedImpact":{"metric":"delivered","delta":62,"unit":"abs"},
  "followUps":["Show bounce hotspots","Compare read rate by country"] }

// benchmark-gap (as used in a SparkleChip)
{ "id":"ins_read_rate_win", "type":"benchmark-gap", "group":"engagement",
  "scope":{"campaignId":"cmp_welcome_wa"}, "metrics":{"readRate":55,"peerAvg":48},
  "summary":"Read rate is 14.5% above peer cohort.",
  "rationale":"Short, personal copy and a familiar sender number drove read uplift…",
  "confidence":"high","severity":"healthy",
  "evidence":[{"id":"ev_read_trend","kind":"trend","title":"Read rate vs peer"}],
  "followUps":["Which segment read most?","Compare against email peers"] }

// recommendation (as used in agent turn 3)
{ "id":"ins_best_next_action", "type":"recommendation", "group":"recommendations",
  "scope":{"campaignId":"cmp_welcome_wa"},
  "metrics":{"estRevenueLift":2300,"estConvLift":8},
  "summary":"Highest-impact move: shorten Step 2 and tighten email→WhatsApp gap to 6h.",
  "rationale":"Addresses the two biggest drags in one change window.",
  "confidence":"high","severity":"action-needed",
  "evidence":[{"id":"ev_flow_funnel","kind":"funnel","title":"Flow funnel"},
              {"id":"ev_seq_compare","kind":"bar","title":"Sequence compare"}],
  "recommendedAction":{"kind":"launch-experiment","label":"Launch A/B on Step 2 + cadence",
                      "description":"50/50 split for 7 days. Primary metric: conversion rate."},
  "estimatedImpact":{"metric":"conversion","delta":8,"unit":"pct"},
  "followUps":["Build me a test plan","What should I change first?"] }
```

The same object powers:

- `InsightCard` (Plan 1 dashboard + Overview tab biggest-win/issue/action cards)
- `RecommendationCard` (Plan 1 Recommendations tab)
- `SparkleChip` + `RightRail` (Plan 2 smart mode)
- `AgentTurn` (Plan 3 scripted turns — each `AgentTurn` carries an `insightId` for grounding)

---

## 4. Metric-universe coverage matrix

Every metric from the original brief §1.2, mapped to which component(s) expose it in each plan.

Legend: ● = surfaced, ◐ = exposed via chip/agent only, ○ = not surfaced in prototype (documented in contract).

| Metric | Plan 1 surface | Plan 2 | Plan 3 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **A. Delivery & quality** | | | |
| Sent | KpiStrip Engagement lens | ● | Scorecard |
| Delivered, Failed | Delivery tab table | ● | ◐ |
| Delivery rate | KpiStrip | AINarrative, chip | Scorecard |
| Failure rate, Failure reason | Delivery tab `BarChart` | ◐ (via `ev_failure_reasons`) | ◐ |
| Read count, Read rate | KpiStrip, Campaign perf tab | ● | Scorecard |
| Read latency | Delivery tab `TrendChart` | ◐ | ◐ |
| Template rejection / pause rate | Campaign perf template table | ◐ | ◐ |
| Quality rating trend | Delivery tab watch banner | chip "Find quality risk" | Agent turn 4 + watchlist |
| Messaging limit utilization | KpiStrip Cost&Quality lens | ● | Scorecard |
| Country-level delivery variation | Delivery tab geo table | ◐ | ◐ |
| **B. Engagement & interaction** | | | |
| Clicks, CTR, unique clickers | Overview ROI block | ◐ | ◐ |
| Interactive click rate, Button / List selection rate | Interaction tab interactive table | chip `ev_interactive` | ◐ |
| Carousel / product card engagement | ○ — stub in schema | ○ | ○ |
| Reply count, Reply rate | Overview replies block + Interaction trend | ● | Scorecard |
| Median time to first reply | KpiStrip Conversation lens | ● | ◐ |
| Two-way conversation rate, depth | Interaction tab conversation depth bar | ◐ | ◐ |
| Business / customer initiated share | ○ — stub | ○ | ○ |
| **C. Flow & task completion** | | | |
| Flow start / completion / timeout / failure rate | Flows tab funnel + diagnostics | ● | Agent turn 1, 2 |
| Step-level drop-off | Flows tab `AdaptiveFunnel` | ● | ◐ |
| Retry rate | Flows tab table | ◐ | ◐ |
| Completion by segment/geo/template version | Flows tab `BarChart` + Audience tab matrix | ● | Agent turn 2 |
| Path from flow completion to conversion | Cross-channel tab | ◐ | ◐ |
| **D. Conversation operations** | | | |
| Bot containment, Human handoff | KpiStrip Conversation lens | chip "Why are handoffs rising?" | ◐ |
| Resolution rate, Avg time to resolution, CSAT | KpiStrip Conversation lens | ◐ | ◐ |
| Intent distribution | Interaction tab `BarChart` | ● | ◐ |
| Unresolved conversation backlog | ○ — stub | ○ | ○ |
| **E. Business outcomes & attribution** | | | |
| Conversion count + rate | KpiStrip Business lens | ● | Scorecard |
| Revenue, Attributed, Assisted | Revenue tab trend + split | ● | Agent turn 5 |
| Revenue per delivered/read/click/reply/completed | Implicit (derivable in `semantic.ts`) | ◐ | ◐ |
| AOV | Revenue tab segment table | ◐ | ◐ |
| Time to conversion | ○ — add `<Histogram>` later | ○ | ○ |
| New vs returning | Revenue tab split | ● | Agent turn 5 |
| Cross-sell / upsell revenue | ○ — stub | ○ | ○ |
| Incremental lift (holdout) | ○ — stub | ○ | ○ |
| **F. Cost & efficiency** | | | |
| Spend, Cost per {delivered,read,click,reply,conversion} | KpiStrip Cost&Quality lens | ◐ | Scorecard |
| Revenue-to-cost ratio | KpiStrip Cost&Quality lens | ◐ | ◐ |
| Projected spend | ○ — add via Forecast API | ○ | ○ |
| **G. Cross channel** | | | |
| WA only / Email only / mixed performance | Cross-channel tab sequence tables | chip "Compare WhatsApp only vs mixed" | Agent turn 5 |
| Sequence order, gap-vs-conversion, fatigue risk | Cross-channel tab | ● | ◐ |
| Channel preference propensity | ○ — stub | ○ | ○ |

Everything marked ○ is in the Insight schema but not yet hooked up — they'd ship in Phase 5 (post-prototype) and use the same object shape.

---

## 5. API / data contracts for the real backend

All endpoints read-only except `POST /actions`. Return shapes match component prop contracts so swapping `lib/mock/*` for `fetch()` is a one-file change per service.

### 5.1 Metrics API

`GET /api/v1/metrics?scope=campaign&id=cmp_welcome_wa&lens=business&dateFrom=2024-11-11&dateTo=2024-12-11`

```jsonc
{
  "scope": {"type":"campaign","id":"cmp_welcome_wa"},
  "lens": "business",
  "kpis": [ /* KpiDef[] — shape matches lib/types.ts */ ],
  "trend": { "revenue": [{"date":"2024-11-11","value":680}, …], "conversions": [ … ] },
  "funnel": [ /* FunnelStage[] */ ]
}
```

### 5.2 Filter metadata API

`GET /api/v1/filters?scope=campaign&id=cmp_welcome_wa`

Returns the option sets for each `FilterBar` control (so the bar can render allowed values dynamically).

### 5.3 Benchmark API

`GET /api/v1/benchmarks?metric=readRate&cohort=peer&industry=personal-services&window=30d` → `{ p25, p50, p75, sample: 248 }`

### 5.4 Anomaly API

`GET /api/v1/anomalies?scope=campaign&id=cmp_welcome_wa` → `Insight[]` with `type: "anomaly"`. Ordered by severity desc, then confidence desc.

### 5.5 Forecast API

`POST /api/v1/forecast` body `{ scope, metric, horizonDays, scenarios: [{ lever: "step2", delta: 0.10 }] }` → `{ baseline, scenarios: [{ lever, projected, lift, confidenceInterval }] }`.

### 5.6 Recommendation API

`GET /api/v1/recommendations?scope=campaign&id=cmp_welcome_wa&limit=10` → `Insight[]` with `recommendedAction` populated, ordered by `estimatedImpact.delta` desc.

### 5.7 Agent conversation API

`POST /api/v1/agent/messages` body `{ campaignId, turns: AgentTurn[], userMessage: string }`. The server response streams `AgentTurn` chunks. The prototype hits `lib/mock/agent-script.ts` today — the contract is designed so swapping to SSE/LLM streaming requires no UI changes in `ConversationCanvas`.

### 5.8 Action execution API

`POST /api/v1/actions` body `{ kind: ActionKind, scope, params }` → `{ actionId, status: "staged"|"applied"|"failed", draftUrl? }`. Every action returns a `draftUrl` so the prototype's Toast confirmation can be upgraded to a "Review draft" link.

### 5.9 Saved views API

`GET /api/v1/saved-views?userId=...` and `POST /api/v1/saved-views` — persists `FilterState + LensId + selectedInsights` as a JSON blob.

### 5.10 Agent memory object

```ts
type AgentMemory = {
  userId: string;
  objective: string;
  priorQuestions: { prompt: string; insightId?: string; timestamp: string }[];
  dismissedAlerts: string[];
  acceptedActions: { actionId: string; timestamp: string; result?: string }[];
  watchlist: string[];  // insight.id
};
```

Read-write. Enables "proactive alerts" — the agent sends a turn at most once per watched signal.

### 5.11 Filter-state contract

Every filter is URL-serializable. Example:

`/whatsapp/report/cmp_welcome_wa/insights?date=last-30d&compare=prior-period&segment=seg_new&geo=IN`

Keys map 1:1 to `FilterState` in `components/filters/FilterBar.tsx`. A shared `lib/filter-url.ts` (TODO — scaffolded for post-prototype) will encode/decode.

---

## 6. Route map

| Route | Purpose | Components loaded |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `/` | Redirect | — |
| `/whatsapp/dashboard` | Plan 1 portfolio | `FilterBar, KpiStrip, TrendChart, AdaptiveFunnel, InsightCard, RankedTable` |
| `/whatsapp/report/[campaignId]` | Plan 1 campaign report | `CampaignHero, ReportTabs (+ 10 tab components)` |
| `/whatsapp/report/[campaignId]/insights` | Plan 2 smart mode | `SmartInsightsView, SparkleChip, RightRail, EvidencePanel` |
| `/whatsapp/report/[campaignId]/agent` | Plan 3 agent mode | `ObjectiveBar, HealthScorecard, ConversationCanvas, EvidenceRail` |

Future routes (in schema, not built): `/whatsapp/segments/[id]`, `/whatsapp/templates/[id]`, `/whatsapp/sequences/[id]`.

---

## 7. Build-sequence recommendation

Mirrors the original brief's §6 but calibrated to the prototype.

1. **Phase 1 — ship Plan 1 to production.** Fastest path to value. Most marketers already expect this shape. Use the prototype's `KpiStrip + AdaptiveFunnel + ReportTabs` as the skeleton.
2. **Phase 2 — layer Plan 2 as the premium experience.** Reuses the same hero, same KPIs, same funnel — only the below-fold changes. This is where differentiation starts. Add the Insight/Recommendation/Anomaly services before shipping the UI.
3. **Phase 3 — Plan 3 for advanced users + proactive mode.** By now the Insight engine is battle-tested. Wire a real LLM behind `ConversationCanvas` using the existing `AgentTurn` contract. The agent-memory object (§5.10) is the make-or-break asset for proactive nudges.

**Shared foundation first, every time.** The failure mode is three divergent surfaces. Lock the Insight schema (§3), the filter-state contract (§5.11), and the severity system (§1.2) early — the UI layer can iterate freely after.

---

## 8. Open questions & trade-offs

| Topic | Decision / open question |
| --- | --- |
| **Graphik Mailchimp** | Proprietary. Used as first font in the stack so Mailchimp-internal renders correctly; public prototype falls back to Inter. For a real marketing page, licensing or a web-font CDN inside Mailchimp infra is required. |
| **Tab fidelity** | Below-fold tabs (Delivery & quality, Interaction & conversation, Audience & geo, Cross channel) get functional layouts in the prototype, not full-pixel density. Flag when upgrading to production. |
| **Agent is scripted** | The prototype's agent replays `lib/mock/agent-script.ts` with typing delays. All evidence and actions are real. Swap with streaming LLM via §5.7; no UI change needed. |
| **Charts** | Recharts chosen for speed + Mailchimp theming via CSS vars. If brand alignment requires hand-rolled SVG (e.g. custom bar end caps, exact Freddie-styled tooltips), budget ~1 extra phase. |
| **Filter-state URL sync** | Scaffolded; not yet implemented in prototype. Recommended before shipping Plan 2 (smart chips need shareable deep links). |
| **`carouselCardEngagement`, `upsellRevenue`, `incrementalLift`, etc.** | In schema, not yet rendered. Low priority until real data exists. |
| **Holdouts** | Spec assumes marketers can run holdouts; UX not built. Defer to Plan 3 once the experiment service is real. |
| **Localization** | Tokens support RTL via CSS logical props; not yet exercised. Add when you get your first EMEA pilot. |
| **Dark mode** | Figma variables include a Mailchimp Dark theme. Tokens are structured for it (`@theme` block in globals.css) but no dark palette has been promoted into the prototype. |

---

## 9. Provenance

**Token capture:** 2026-04-18 via the Figma REST API. File `oVVMotC92NoabJa6hBW3cp` node `2664:3994`. Colors resolved from actual rendered node fills across 12,000+ descendant nodes; typography and radii from TEXT and FRAME nodes in the Mailchimp theme layer. The Fusion/Oasis collection aliases resolve to the primitives listed in §1.1.

**Evidence:** full capture data is in `/tmp/figma-cap/` (node_deep.json, variables.json) should you need to audit any token value. The Figma personal access token used for capture has been flagged to the user for revocation.
